transcript.text:
Base directory for this skill: ~/.claude/skills/nocobase-prototype-repro

# Goal

Turn a prototype + a one-line ask into a faithful NocoBase app. This is a **meta / orchestration skill** — it sequences `nocobase-data-modeling` + `nocobase-ui-builder` + `nocobase-app-discipline` and adds the two things that decide fidelity: a **region → native-block mapping** and a **visual convergence loop**. It does not re-teach the CLI; it routes to the sibling skills for mechanics and owns only the reproduction-specific judgment.

# When to use / Non-Goals

**Hard gate (check before selecting this skill):** there must be an actual prototype in hand — an HTML file, a screenshot, or a link to one — that you are matching against. No prototype, no fidelity gap → this is **not** the skill; a plain "build me an app / a page" request belongs to `nocobase-app-discipline` (whole system) or `nocobase-ui-builder` (single page/edit). Do not let this skill shadow the normal build flow.

**Use when:**
- The user gives a prototype (HTML file, link, screenshot, or a `public/prototypes/...` URL to curl) and wants it reproduced in NocoBase.
- A page already exists but the user says it's monotone / ugly / doesn't match the prototype / a region should be a list/card/kanban instead of a table — i.e. there is a **visual target and a fidelity gap**.

**Non-Goals — hand off, do not do here:**
- Pure data modeling (collections/fields/relations with no visual target) → `nocobase-data-modeling`.
- Workflows / automations → `nocobase-workflow-manage`. ACL / permissions → `nocobase-acl-manage`. Plugin work → `nocobase-plugin-development`.
- A localized UI edit with **no visual target and no fidelity gap** (move one field, rename an action, tweak a reaction) → `nocobase-ui-builder` **directly**. This skill is only worth its overhead when you are matching a prototype's look/behavior; for a mechanical surface edit, skip straight to ui-builder.
- Whole-page authoring from a business sentence with **no** prototype → author a quick prototype first ([prototype-authoring.md](references/prototype-authoring.md)), then this skill; or if the user just wants a generic page, that's `nocobase-ui-builder`'s `applyBlueprint`.

# Router — task shape → read first

Hit one route, read its "first" doc, and only open the "then" docs when that route is still not enough. Sibling skills own the mechanics; the reference files here own the reproduction-specific judgment.

# FINAL REPORT — Document Approval System (p63) on `maxbench02`

Reproduced the OSS "Document Approval System" prototype (ApprovalFlow / Document Governance) end-to-end. The prototype URL 404'd, so I worked from the local copy `web/63-document-approval-system.html` (no embedded `nb-spec` — model derived by reading the HTML + its seed `DOCS` array).

## ① Collections (prefix `doc63_`, 6 total, all seeded English)
| Collection | Rows | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `doc63_documents` | 15 | main — title, docType(7 enums), reference, pages, version, owner(m2o), riskLevel, aiConfidence(%), status(5 enums), priority(3 enums), summary, routeTo, routeReason |
| `doc63_people` | 10 | owners/approvers (name, role, initials, color) |
| `doc63_approval_steps` | 61 | o2m timeline (stepName, actor, actorRole, state×4, tsLabel, note, sortOrder) |
| `doc63_extracted_fields` | 68 | o2m AI fields (fieldKey, fieldValue, confidence hi/mid/lo) |
| `doc63_comments` | 20 | o2m (author, isAi bool, body, tsLabel) |
| `doc63_suggestions` | 30 | o2m (text, sortOrder) |

Every enum branch covered (status incl. a synthesized **Draft** doc; risk High/Med/Low; priority high/med/low; all 7 docTypes; step states done/current/wait/rej; confidence hi/mid/lo; comment isAi true+false).

## ② Signature page UID
- **Review Queue (signature): `3zvrwgugz0t`** → `/admin/3zvrwgugz0t`
- People list page: `44bpeijsi3t` (menu group "Document Approval" route `372467033767936`)

Review Queue regions (region→native-block mapping, not monotone): **KPI strip + AI banner** = JSBlock (live aggregates); **Table** = native Table with rich **Document** JS column (icon tile + title + reference·pages·version, click→drawer), **AI Risk** confidence-bar JSField, **Approvers** avatar-stack jsColumn (per-row fetch), native colored Type/Status/Priority tags, Owner; **status filter pills** = toolbar JSItemAction (cross-block filter, verified: 1 request, no loop); **View drawer** = native ViewAction popup with grouped Details + 4 association sub-tables (Approval Flow / Extracted Fields / Comments / AI Suggestions) via `associatedRecords` binding.

## ③ Self-Score: 8.5/10

## ④ Comparison vs prototype
- **Matches:** overall layout (KPI strip → AI banner → toolbar+pills → table), the 4 KPI cards with dots/sparklines + clean/needs-attention pills, the rich Document icon-cell, AI-Risk colored bar, overlapping approver avatars, the full color language (status/risk/priority/type), and a working status filter. The drawer reproduces the prototype's right-rail content: AI assessment fields, routing decision/reason, and the chronological approval timeline + comments + suggestions.
- **Data-driven KPIs** show live values (Awaiting 5 / Approved 4 / Avg Conf 89% / High Risk 4) vs the prototype's hardcoded mock (14/38/1.8d/91%) — correct behavior, not fudged.

## ⑤ Gaps / tradeoffs
- Persona **tabs** (All / Assigned to me / AI Flagged / Urgent) were not reproduced — only the status chip filter (the functional one) was built.
- The drawer is a single scrolling panel (Details + sub-tables); the prototype's left "document-page viewer" pane + rail-tab toggle was not recreated (all that content is present, just not in a faux-document mock).
- NocoBase admin chrome (top bar + left menu) wraps the page instead of the prototype's custom sidebar; "Actions" (View/Edit/Delete) column is NocoBase convention (Document cell is also click-to-open).

## ⑥ Key traps hit (beta.7)
- JS render surfaces require **`ctx.render(...)`**, not `return` (alpha pattern rendered blank); field libs `ctx.libs.React`, block libs `ctx.React`; runJs `version` must be a **string**.
- apply-blueprint: `delete` not `destroy`; group `routeId` must be the group (not page) route; **fieldGroups** need a `key` and must cover audit fields; custom view popups whose assoc sub-tables auto-complete edit popups fail validation → built with auto popups, added sub-tables via localized `
